description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging cylindrical shape, constructed from dark brown wooden staves. The barrel is reinforced by three prominent black metal hoops that encircle its body, providing structural integrity and a traditional appearance. The wood surface shows subtle grain patterns and a matte finish, suggesting it is aged or weathered. The top and bottom are flat, with the top surface appearing solid and seamless, indicating it is currently closed. There are no visible handles, spouts, or openings, and the exterior is free from any labels or markings. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban environments such as alleys, storage areas, or near taverns and markets.
description: A dark wooden barrel with black metal hoops, bulging sides, and a closed top, showing a weathered, matte finish.
description_view_0: Front view of the barrel showing the bulging sides, three black metal hoops, and a flat, closed top.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing the wood grain and the curvature of the barrel, with all three metal hoops visible.
description_view_2: Another side view with similar features, highlighting the seamless top and the continuous metal bands.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, showing the same structural details and the absence of handles or markings.
